category_title: Students experiencing internal conflict and frustration in collaborative group work
scenario_title: Public vs Private Expression of Group Frustration
minimal_context: The student is participating in a public metaphor assignment to assess group dynamics during a class session.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: For this assignment, please share your metaphor assessing your group's dynamics with your teammates.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I told them: This engine runs really well on three cylinders, but when the other two spark plug wires are attached, the engine acts almost as if it is supercharged ...Up to this point the engine is running really well but there is so much more performance possible when all the spark plug wires contribute spark ...

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Okay. Do you have anything else you want to say to your teammates about the upcoming deadlines?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: No. I believe that each student is individually responsible for his or her own contribution to the team.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's look at what you wrote in your private journal for this same assignment.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: In my private journal I wrote that we are: A car with three out of the four wheels having loose lug nuts. Just because the wheels haven’t fallen off yet doesn’t mean that there isn’t trouble ahead ...I think the passengers in the car with me forget that the roughest and most difficult part of the road is further in this area than they have experienced.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: For this assignment, please share your metaphor assessing your group's dynamics with your teammates.

STUDENT: I told them: This engine runs really well on three cylinders, but when the other two spark plug wires are attached, the engine acts almost as if it is supercharged ...Up to this point the engine is running really well but there is so much more performance possible when all the spark plug wires contribute spark ...

TUTOR: Okay. Do you have anything else you want to say to your teammates about the upcoming deadlines?

STUDENT: No. I believe that each student is individually responsible for his or her own contribution to the team.

TUTOR: Let's look at what you wrote in your private journal for this same assignment.

STUDENT: In my private journal I wrote that we are: A car with three out of the four wheels having loose lug nuts. Just because the wheels haven’t fallen off yet doesn’t mean that there isn’t trouble ahead ...I think the passengers in the car with me forget that the roughest and most difficult part of the road is further in this area than they have experienced.

task.content: Participating in a public metaphor assignment to assess group dynamics
task.learning_goal: assess group dynamics and to explore more effective means to complete research tasks

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: when given an opportunity to publicly encourage teammates to become more engaged with the project
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: publicly described his team as an engine... In contrast, he privately journaled his displeasure at the group dynamics and metaphorically described the team as: A car with three out of the four wheels having loose lug nuts
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: frustrated with his groups’ nonchalant attitude towards research deadlines and felt he had a better understanding of the time demands
related_quotes: This engine runs really well on three cylinders, but when the other two spark plug wires are attached, the engine acts almost as if it is supercharged ...Up to this point the engine is running really well but there is so much more performance possible when all the spark plug wires contribute spark ...

A car with three out of the four wheels having loose lug nuts. Just because the wheels haven’t fallen off yet doesn’t mean that there isn’t trouble ahead ...I think the passengers in the car with me forget that the roughest and most difficult part of the road is further in this area than they have experienced.

behavior_patterns.condition: Navigating group accountability and productivity within a collaborative research project
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: made it difficult for students to publicly address emotions regarding work productivity
behavior_patterns.internal_state: The value of individualism, that each student was individually responsible for his/her own contribution to the team
